data.description:
## Description

**What**: Open a deliberate-violation test PR against this repo's CI workflow with a known security-overlay-detectable issue (e.g., hardcoded API secret in a tracked file) and verify in-session that (a) the security overlay fires, (b) the finding is recorded as SECURITY_OVERLAY_TRIGGERED in reviewer-findings.json, (c) the CI llm-review job exits non-zero, and (d) gh pr merge --auto remains queued (not executing) until the violation is fixed.

**Why**: SC6 of Epic C requires this end-to-end demonstration to prove the parity-uplifted CI gate actually catches what it claims to catch. Without this story, the architecture is built but unproven.

**Scope**:
- IN: open a test PR with a deliberate violation; observe security overlay finding; observe CI exit non-zero; observe gh pr checks showing failed status; observe gh pr merge --auto queued without firing; close + delete the test branch in the same session after verification.
- OUT: host-project E2E (Story 7); overlay implementation (Story 3).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a deliberate-violation test PR has been opened and the security overlay agent has produced a SECURITY_OVERLAY_TRIGGERED finding in the structured reviewer-findings.json artifact.
  ← Satisfies: "Test PR with deliberate violation (e.g., hardcoded API secret in a tracked file) triggers security overlay and CI exits non-zero with a SECURITY_OVERLAY_TRIGGERED finding in the structured artifact"
- When this story is complete, the CI llm-review job for that PR has exited non-zero (verified via gh pr checks showing the failed status).
  ← Satisfies: "CI exits non-zero with a SECURITY_OVERLAY_TRIGGERED finding ... verified in-session"
- When this story is complete, gh pr merge --auto remains queued without firing as long as the violation is present (verified in-session).
  ← Satisfies: required-checks blocking on the security overlay finding
- When this story is complete, the test PR is closed without merging and its branch deleted in the same session — verified via gh pr view --state all showing state CLOSED.
  ← Satisfies: test PR lifecycle hygiene
- unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] The deliberate violation should be chosen carefully to trigger the security overlay reliably without being a real credential. Use a clearly synthetic API-key-shaped string (e.g., "sk-FAKE-TEST-ONLY-deliberate-violation-for-test-1234") in a comment or string literal in a tracked file. If GitHub's secret-scanning push protection blocks the push, choose a different overlay-triggering pattern (e.g., insecure deserialization, SQL injection pattern).

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a project maintainer, I can verify on a real PR that the security overlay catches a deliberate violation and blocks merge end-to-end
